title: Ileostylus micranthus
taxonomy: Ileostylus micranthus is classified under the family Rubiaceae and order Lamiales. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The species Ileostylus micranthus is further categorized under the family Rubiaceae, subfamily Rubioidae. The Ileostylus micranthus is native to the tropical regions of South America and belongs to the genus Ileostylus. The species is often found growing in the moist, subtropical forests of the continent.

intro: Ileostylus micranthus, also known as the small-styled Stylo, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, traditionally used in African folk medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Ileostylus micranthus include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ileostylus micranthus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
